title: BUEPT: C1 Inversion with Negative Adverbials
explanation: Advanced C1 practice focusing on Inversion with Negative Adverbials. Essential for BUEPT academic precision.

questions.q: Hardly ___ I started when it began to rain.
If I ___ you, I wouldn't do that.
It is essential that he ___ on time.
Should it rain, we ___ stay home.
Under no circumstances ___ you open the door.
I'd rather you ___ here yesterday.
Not only ___ he rich, but also talented.
Little ___ he know that it was a trap.
So ___ was the weather that stayed in.
If only I ___ her the truth.
Analyze the following: 'Given the circumstances, he ___ have acted differently.'
Identify the error: 'The research, documenting by several experts, was comprehensive.'
Select the most academic form: 'The results ___ the hypothesis.'
Choose the correct transition: '___ the initial setbacks, the project was a success.'
Which sentence uses a passive voice correctly?
Seldom ___ such a comprehensive review in this field.
The proposal was rejected, ___ its methodological rigor.
The speaker’s claim was so tentative that it could hardly be said to ___ a conclusion.
Had it not been for the pilot study, the flaw ___ unnoticed.
The report is written in a style ___ to minimize ambiguity.
